section.title: Grades
section.p: General PCC Grading Guidelines are found at .

paragraphs.title: Attendance/Engagement
paragraphs.p: Attending class is worth 4% of your final grade. If you have a reasonable circumstance where you need to miss class, including being sick, tell me about it. I will ask you to do a few small things and then you can recover the attendance credit for that day.

paragraphs.title: Online Homework
paragraphs.p: You have online homework for each section using . See for details. The due date for each assignment is posted in , but it will always be Sunday evening at 10:00 for full credit. Usually, more than one section will be due at the same time. You should start working on these exercises soon after the corresponding class session.
Collectively, all the online homework is worth 20% of your final grade.

paragraphs.title: Lab Exercise Write-Ups
paragraphs.p: Each week you have an assignment of exercises from the PCC Lab Manual to complete. See for the options on how you can be graded for this. Collectively, these assignments are worth 15% of your grade.
The due dates for these assignments are Tuesday mornings. (Either in class Monday, to my office on Tuesday morning, or scanned and posted in D2L by Tuesday morning.)

Please note that these assignments will only be scored once. There is not an opportunity to improve your score once an assignment is graded. But you are welcome to bring me a draft of your work outside of class, where we can informally look it over together to catch any significant issues before you turn it in.

I will be looking for a few things:
correctness of your responses
readability, including the layout of your responses and if your responses are convincing
correct use of math notation and vocabulary
Also your write-up should connect the questions to your answers. If it's simply a list of answers with no context, it is not useful to you as a study tool later. So you should either include each question preceding each answer, or you should have a clear system for connecting the questions to the answers.

paragraphs.title: Exams
paragraphs.p: There is a final exam during finals week that is worth 25% of your grade. There are also two midterm exams, each worth 18% of your grade. Please see the for dates.
The exams will be in-class. For each exam I will allocate an appropriate amount of time, and remaining time will be used for lab assignment progress.

paragraphs.title: Grading Options
paragraphs.p: Students are solely responsible for adding, dropping, or withdrawing from any class, as well as for selecting their desired grading option. Please review PCC Registration Policy for more information.

paragraphs.title: Letter Grades
paragraphs.p: Your letter grade will be assigned based on the weighted average of the items above.
